methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $15M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $150M - $500M.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

private.name: Stark Defence
private.rationale: Same sector (Defense & Security), same stage (Series A), post-money $500M

private.name: Vatn Systems

private.rationale: Same sector (Defense & Security), same stage (Series A), post-money $150M

data.narrative:
SiFly is a Series A-stage Defense & Security company that has raised $15M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comparables universe of 10 peers: 2 public companies (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private firms, including same-stage Defense & Security benchmarks Stark Defence ($500M), Vatn Systems ($300M), and Ares Industries ($150M).

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, using the $15M total raised as a proxy for post-money valuation and applying a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical for Series A advancement, yields $30M. Peer-median analysis across the three same-sector, same-stage comparables produces a $300M implied valuation, with a range spanning $150M to $500M. The median of the three peers is $300M.

Triangulating these methods produces a valuation range of $26M (low) to $345M (median), with a central point of $165M. The substantial width of this range—spanning more than one order of magnitude—reflects material uncertainty in the peer-median approach, which is sensitive to stage classification and sector definition. Stage-progression analysis anchors the lower bound, while peer comparables establish the ceiling. Key uncertainties include customer concentration, technical differentiation relative to named peers, and revenue traction. Further diligence on unit economics and customer contracts would narrow this range materially.
